pros: Zero commission on sales across every plan
15-day free trial with no credit card required
30-day money-back guarantee
Free domain included for the first year on annual plans
Rated 4.7/5 on Capterra with 600+ reviews
cons: No AI features are mentioned on the site
No permanent free plan — only a 15-day trial
Standalone Client Gallery is a separate add-on plan rather than included by default

faqs.question: Does Pixpa offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 15-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Pixpa charge commission on sales?
faqs.answer: No, all plans are zero-commission on prints, products, services, and digital files.

faqs.question: Who is Pixpa built for?
faqs.answer: Photographers, artists, designers, illustrators, models, and other creative professionals.

faqs.question: Does Pixpa support client proofing?
faqs.answer: Yes, private client galleries support proofing, comments, and multi-user favoriting.

faqs.question: What languages does Pixpa support?
faqs.answer: The site is available in English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Dutch, Italian, Polish, and Russian.

use_cases.title: Photographer portfolio with client proofing
use_cases.description: Photographers can deliver private galleries for client selection and approval.

use_cases.title: Selling prints and digital files
use_cases.description: Creatives can sell prints and downloads without losing a commission cut.

use_cases.title: Creative professional website
use_cases.description: Illustrators, architects, and designers can showcase work with a designer-made template.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pixpa?
paragraphs: Pixpa is a website builder built specifically for photographers and other creative professionals, combining a portfolio site, private client galleries with proofing, a zero-commission store, and a blog in one platform.
It's backed by a 15-day free trial, a 30-day money-back guarantee, and annual plans starting at $5.40/month.

enrichment.short_description: Format is a website builder for photographers and creatives, offering portfolio themes, client galleries, and a commission-free store.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Format is a portfolio website builder for photographers and creatives with client galleries and a commission-free store, plans from $10/month.

features.title: 90+ themes
features.description: Professionally designed themes with extensive customization options.

features.title: Flex Block layout builder
features.description: A drag-and-drop tool for building custom page layouts.

features.title: Client galleries and file transfers
features.description: Share proofing galleries and transfer files with clients.

features.title: Commission-free store
features.description: Sell products, services, and digital downloads without a commission cut.

features.title: Hosted video
features.description: Upload and host up to 120 minutes of video directly on higher plans.

features.title: Multilingual sites via Weglot
features.description: Translate a site into multiple languages using the Weglot integration.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Commission-free online store on Pro plans and above
90+ themes built specifically for photography and creative portfolios
Weglot integration enables multilingual sites
cons: No permanent free plan — only a 14-day trial
Basic plan caps pages at 10 and has no online store or free custom domain
No AI features are mentioned on the site

faqs.question: Does Format offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Is there a commission on store sales?
faqs.answer: No, the online store on Pro plans and above is commission-free.

faqs.question: Who is Format for?
faqs.answer: Photographers, filmmakers, illustrators, artists, designers, and other creative professionals.

faqs.question: Can I host video on Format?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pro Plus plan supports up to 120 minutes of hosted video.

faqs.question: Can I translate my Format site?
faqs.answer: Yes, via the built-in Weglot integration.

use_cases.title: Photography portfolio with client proofing
use_cases.description: Photographers can share proofing galleries and transfer files to clients.

use_cases.title: Selling prints and digital downloads
use_cases.description: Creatives can run a commission-free store from their portfolio site.

use_cases.title: Multilingual creative site
use_cases.description: Creatives with an international audience can translate their site via Weglot.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Format?
paragraphs: Format is a website builder built for photographers and creative professionals, offering 90+ portfolio themes, client galleries with file transfers, and a commission-free online store.
Plans scale by image storage, hosted video minutes, and number of client galleries, with a 14-day free trial available on all paid tiers.

enrichment.short_description: Webnode is an AI-assisted website builder for small businesses and freelancers, with booking, invoicing, and a permanent free plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Webnode is an AI website builder for small businesses with online booking, invoicing, and SEO tools, plus a permanent free plan.

features.title: AI website generator
features.description: Generates a starting site instantly and can create and edit content with AI.

features.title: Drag-and-drop editor
features.description: Customize the AI-generated site manually without code.

features.title: Online booking
features.description: Built-in appointment scheduling for service businesses.

features.title: Invoicing and payments
features.description: Create invoices and accept payments directly through the site.

features.title: SEO tools
features.description: Built-in tools to improve search engine visibility.

features.title: Multilingual websites
features.description: Build sites that support multiple languages.

pros: Permanent free plan for building and hosting a website indefinitely
Founded in 2008, giving it a long operating track record
AI generator creates a starting site and content automatically
Built-in booking and invoicing suit service-based small businesses
cons: Free plan keeps Webnode branding and likely limits custom domains and bandwidth
Exact paid plan pricing was not directly verifiable from the official pricing page at the time of writing
No native iOS/Android app is mentioned, only web-based editing

faqs.question: When was Webnode founded?
faqs.answer: Webnode was founded in 2008.

faqs.question: Is Webnode free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Webnode offers a free plan for building and hosting a website indefinitely; premium plans unlock custom domains, more bandwidth, and branding removal.

faqs.question: Does Webnode use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, its AI website generator can create content, edit pages, and build a site from a prompt.

faqs.question: Can I take bookings and invoice clients on Webnode?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes online booking and invoice creation with payment processing.

faqs.question: How many languages does Webnode support?
faqs.answer: The platform advertises support for 25+ languages, including English, Spanish, French, German, Chinese, and Japanese.

use_cases.title: Service business booking site
use_cases.description: Contractors, cleaners, and plumbers can take appointments directly through their site.

use_cases.title: Freelancer invoicing
use_cases.description: Freelancers can invoice and get paid through their Webnode site.

use_cases.title: Personal blog
use_cases.description: Individuals can start a free personal blog without any cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Webnode?
paragraphs: Webnode, founded in 2008, is an AI-assisted website builder aimed at small businesses, contractors, and freelancers, with an AI generator that creates and edits site content from a prompt.
It includes online booking, invoicing and payments, SEO tools, and support for 25+ interface languages, with a free plan available indefinitely alongside premium tiers.

enrichment.short_description: A no-code, click-to-edit website builder with built-in e-commerce, blogging, and analytics for entrepreneurs and creatives.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Strikingly is a no-code website builder with click-to-edit design, a built-in store, blog, and analytics for creatives and entrepreneurs.

features.title: Click-to-edit visual editor
features.description: Click any element on the page to edit it directly and publish instantly, with no code or design experience required.

features.title: Simple Store
features.description: Sell products with built-in e-commerce, connecting to PayPal or Stripe, plus coupons, shipping, and digital goods.

features.title: Simple Blog
features.description: Publish blog posts that each get their own dedicated page, built directly into the site editor.

features.title: Built-in analytics
features.description: Every site includes easy-to-read visitor analytics with no separate integration needed.

features.title: Sign-up, contact, and membership tools
features.description: Add sign-up forms, contact forms, live chat, newsletters, and gated membership content.

features.title: Social feed embedding
features.description: Display Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram feeds directly on a site.

features.title: Free HTTPS
features.description: All published sites get HTTPS automatically at no extra cost.

features.title: AI Site Builder and AI Logo Maker
features.description: AI-assisted tools are available for generating a starting site and a logo.

pros: No coding or design skills required to publish a working site
Built-in e-commerce and blogging without needing extra plugins
Free HTTPS and analytics included on every site
Available in 18 interface languages and has a dedicated iPhone app
cons: Custom domain connection requires upgrading from the free plan
Mobile editing app is iPhone-only, with no Android app mentioned on the site
Detailed paid-plan pricing isn't published in a simple static table on the pricing page

faqs.question: Does Strikingly support e-commerce?
faqs.answer: Yes, through the built-in Simple Store, which connects to PayPal or Stripe and supports coupons, shipping, and digital goods.

faqs.question: Do I need coding skills to use Strikingly?
faqs.answer: No, Strikingly is a no-code, click-to-edit builder aimed at people with no design or development background.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Strikingly offers an iPhone app for building and editing sites on the go.

faqs.question: Does Strikingly include a blog?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Simple Blog feature is built into the editor and gives each post its own page.

use_cases.title: One-page portfolios and landing pages
use_cases.description: Creatives and freelancers use Strikingly's templates to launch a mobile-optimized site in under 30 minutes.

use_cases.title: Small online stores
use_cases.description: Businesses sell a single product or a full catalog using the built-in Simple Store with PayPal/Stripe checkout.

use_cases.title: Blogging and audience building
use_cases.description: Writers publish updates through the Simple Blog and grow an audience with newsletters and sign-up forms.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Strikingly?
paragraphs: Strikingly is a website builder built around a click-to-edit interface: users click directly on any element of a template to change it, without touching code or a separate design tool.
Beyond the site editor, it bundles a store (Simple Store), a blog (Simple Blog), sign-up and contact forms, membership gating, and free built-in analytics and HTTPS on every published site.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Strikingly is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at entrepreneurs, creatives, and small businesses who want a simple, mobile-optimized site or one-page store without hiring a developer or designer.

enrichment.short_description: A free, wizard-style website builder with built-in hosting, SEO tools, and an online store option, with custom domains as a paid add-on.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SITE123 is a free website builder with built-in hosting and SEO tools; custom domains and stores are available on paid plans from $10.8/mo.

features.title: Free-forever core plan
features.description: The base plan is free indefinitely, with no credit card required to sign up, and includes hosting and the editor.

features.title: Guided website editor
features.description: A structured, wizard-style editor where users upload content into pre-built layouts rather than a traditional drag-and-drop canvas.

features.title: Built-in SEO tools
features.description: SEO settings are built into the editor to help pages be indexed and found in search engines.

features.title: Free hosting on CDN infrastructure
features.description: Sites are hosted on globally distributed CDN servers to reduce load time for visitors.

features.title: Online store
features.description: Users can create an online store and sell products globally directly from their site.

features.title: Custom domain connection
features.description: A custom domain can be connected on a paid plan starting as low as $10.8/month on an annual, prepaid plan.

features.title: Royalty-free image bank
features.description: An integrated image bank with royalty-free photos is available for use in site design.

features.title: 24/7 live chat support
features.description: Live chat support is available at any time to help with building and managing the site.

pros: Genuinely free core plan with no credit card required and no time limit
Built-in SEO tools and CDN-based free hosting included from the start
24/7 live chat support plus a 14-day money-back guarantee on paid plans
No limit on the number of websites per account
cons: Custom domain and full e-commerce features require a paid upgrade
Editor is more template/wizard-driven than a fully flexible drag-and-drop builder
Sites cannot be downloaded — SITE123 is a closed, proprietary hosting platform

faqs.question: Is SITE123 really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the product is offered at no cost, including hosting and the full editor, with no payment or trial commitment required.

faqs.question: How much does a custom domain cost?
faqs.answer: Connecting a custom domain starts as low as $10.8/month if paid upfront on an annual plan.

faqs.question: How many websites can I create?
faqs.answer: There is no limit — a single account can create and manage multiple websites.

faqs.question: Do I need design skills to use SITE123?
faqs.answer: No, users upload content and pick from ready-made layouts rather than designing from scratch.

use_cases.title: First business website
use_cases.description: Small businesses use the guided editor to get a site online in under an hour without design help.

use_cases.title: Basic online store
use_cases.description: Sellers set up products and start selling online using the built-in store tools.

use_cases.title: Personal or portfolio sites
use_cases.description: Non-technical individuals build a free personal site or portfolio using pre-made layouts.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SITE123?
paragraphs: SITE123 is a website builder centered on a guided, wizard-style editor: rather than free-form drag-and-drop, users select a website type and upload their own content into ready-made layouts.
The core plan is free indefinitely with built-in hosting, SEO tools, and support for an online store, while a custom domain and extra storage/bandwidth are paid upgrades.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SITE123 is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at non-technical users and small businesses who want the fastest possible path to a working website without design or coding skills.

enrichment.short_description: A German website and online store builder offering both a simple no-code editor and a code-customizable Creator editor.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jimdo is a German website and online store builder with a free plan, a simple editor, and a code-customizable Creator editor for more control.

features.title: Two editors: Website Builder and Creator
features.description: A simple no-code website/store builder plus a Creator editor with coding features for custom navigation, stores, and design elements.

features.title: Online Store Builder
features.description: A dedicated builder for setting up an online store, separate from the general website builder.

features.title: Online booking
features.description: Customers can take appointments directly through the website via built-in booking tools.

features.title: Smart forms
features.description: Contact forms can be created for any occasion directly in the editor.

features.title: Business listings (Jimdo Local)
features.description: Manages business listings on Google, Facebook, and other platforms from one place.

features.title: Social selling integration
features.description: Products and content can be promoted and sold directly on Facebook and Instagram.

features.title: Warning-proof legal texts
features.description: Generates privacy policy and terms text intended to meet legal disclosure requirements.

pros: Long operating history — in business since 2007 with over 32 million Jimdo websites created
Offers both a simple editor and a code-customizable Creator editor for teams that want more control
Built-in tools for bookings, forms, and legal pages beyond basic site design
Free plan available, with the option to upgrade at any time
cons: No customer service phone hotline — support is via help center, live chat, or email only
Paying month-to-month costs more than committing to a 12- or 24-month term
Choosing between the Website Builder and Creator editors upfront matters, since their feature sets differ

faqs.question: Can I start free and upgrade later?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can start with a free website and upgrade to a paid plan at any time from the site dashboard.

faqs.question: Can I connect my own domain?
faqs.answer: Yes, premium plans support transferring an existing domain to Jimdo or connecting it while keeping it registered elsewhere.

faqs.question: How do I pay for a plan?
faqs.answer: You can pay monthly, or save money by paying upfront for a 12- or 24-month term.

faqs.question: Can I cancel my contract?
faqs.answer: Consumers get a 14-day right of withdrawal where a statutory right applies; this does not apply to business accounts.

use_cases.title: Local business websites
use_cases.description: Restaurants, coaches, and practices use Jimdo to combine a simple site with online booking and business listings.

use_cases.title: Small online stores
use_cases.description: Sellers use the Online Store Builder together with Facebook/Instagram selling integration.

use_cases.title: Custom-coded sites
use_cases.description: Teams that need more design control use the Creator editor to hand-code navigation and layout.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jimdo?
paragraphs: Jimdo is a German website and online store builder, in operation since 2007, that offers two distinct editors: a simple, guided Website/Store Builder and a more advanced Creator editor with coding features.
Beyond page design, it includes booking tools, smart forms, business listing management for Google and Facebook, and legal-text generation for privacy policies and terms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jimdo is for
paragraphs: It suits local businesses and solopreneurs who want a fast, low-maintenance site or store, as well as developers who want the option to hand-code parts of the site through the Creator editor.

enrichment.short_description: A free publishing platform for paid newsletters, podcasts, and video, where writers keep 90% of subscription revenue.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Substack is a free platform for paid newsletters, podcasts, and video where writers keep 90% of subscriber revenue and own their list.

features.title: Free publishing platform
features.description: Writers can start a publication with no upfront platform fee and no contract to sign.

features.title: Built-in paid subscriptions
features.description: Writers set a subscription price and readers can pay directly through Substack; the writer keeps 90% of that revenue.

features.title: Built-in reader network
features.description: More than half of new subscribers come from Substack's built-in network and recommendations rather than outside promotion.

features.title: Multi-format publishing
features.description: Supports written posts as well as podcasts, video, and live video, not just text newsletters.

features.title: Full ownership of list and payments
features.description: Writers retain ownership of their intellectual property, mailing list, and subscriber payments.

features.title: Substack app
features.description: A dedicated app is available for reading and managing publications.

pros: No cost to start publishing or to launch a paid subscription business
Large existing reader network that surfaces new subscribers through recommendations
Writers keep full ownership of their subscriber list, content, and payouts
Supports podcasts and video in addition to written newsletters
cons: Substack takes a 10% cut of paid subscription revenue, on top of separate payment-processing fees
Built primarily around newsletter/post publishing rather than general-purpose website design
Discovery relies partly on Substack's own network, meaning growth is tied to the platform

faqs.question: Is Substack free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's free to start and free to leave, with no upfront platform fee, tiers, or contract.

faqs.question: How does Substack make money?
faqs.answer: It takes 10% of a writer's paid subscription revenue; the remaining 90% goes to the writer.

faqs.question: Can I publish more than text newsletters?
faqs.answer: Yes, Substack supports podcasts, video, and live video alongside written posts.

faqs.question: Do I own my subscriber list?
faqs.answer: Yes, writers retain ownership of their intellectual property, mailing list, and subscriber payments.

use_cases.title: Paid newsletters
use_cases.description: Independent writers and journalists monetize their writing directly through reader subscriptions.

use_cases.title: Paid podcasts
use_cases.description: Podcasters use Substack's subscription tools to charge for exclusive episodes.

use_cases.title: Niche expertise publications
use_cases.description: Finance writers, food writers, and other subject-matter experts build a direct paying audience.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Substack?
paragraphs: Substack is a publishing platform where writers and creators distribute newsletters, podcasts, and video, with built-in tools for charging readers a subscription fee.
It's free to start, and Substack's revenue comes from taking a 10% cut of paid subscription revenue rather than charging a platform subscription fee.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Substack is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at independent writers, journalists, podcasters, and niche experts who want to build a direct, paid relationship with their audience without giving up ownership of their list or content.

enrichment.short_description: A competitor-monitoring and pipeline-intelligence platform that surfaces named contacts in active sales cycles with your competitors.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Letterdrop is a competitor-monitoring platform that surfaces named contacts in live competitor sales cycles for B2B revenue teams.

features.title: Competitor sales-cycle monitoring
features.description: Tracks up to 3-5 named competitors and surfaces the specific contacts actively talking to them, with a confidence score.

features.title: Daily signal delivery
features.description: Delivers signals daily to Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Apollo, Outreach, Clay, or a spreadsheet, with a recommended outreach play attached.

features.title: Fast time-to-value
features.description: First signals arrive within 10 days, with no implementation project required.

features.title: Native CRM and outreach integrations
features.description: Integrates natively with Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, Fathom, Circleback, Google Sheets, and Zapier.

features.title: MCP integration with ChatGPT and Claude
features.description: Connects via Model Context Protocol so AI assistants can query Letterdrop data and take actions inside the platform.

features.title: SOC 2 compliant data handling
features.description: Customer data is encrypted in transit and at rest, and is not used to train Letterdrop's models.

pros: Surfaces named contacts in live competitor sales cycles, not just anonymous account-level research signals
Delivers signals directly into existing CRM and outreach tools without a new system of record
First signals arrive within 10 days with no implementation project
SOC 2 compliant and does not use customer data to train models
cons: No public self-serve pricing — plans require a demo or sales conversation
Best suited to companies with 50+ employees and an established outbound motion
Stated competitor-signal accuracy is around 30% on average, so not every surfaced contact converts

faqs.question: What does Letterdrop do?
faqs.answer: It identifies contacts who are actively starting sales cycles with your competitors, tracking public signals to surface live conversations before a vendor is chosen.

faqs.question: How is Letterdrop different from 6sense or Bombora?
faqs.answer: 6sense and Bombora track account-level content research; Letterdrop tracks contacts in live conversations forming with specific named competitors.

faqs.question: What does Letterdrop integrate with?
faqs.answer: Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Apollo.io, Outreach, Clay, Gong, Fathom, Circleback, Google Sheets, and Zapier, plus ChatGPT and Claude via MCP.

faqs.question: How accurate is Letterdrop's competitor monitoring?
faqs.answer: It identifies contacts at roughly 30% accuracy on average for active competitor sales cycles, and up to about 1 in 2 in some verticals.

use_cases.title: Intercepting competitor deals
use_cases.description: Sales-led B2B companies use Letterdrop to reach named contacts before a competitor closes the deal.
use_cases.audience: VPs of Sales and RevOps leaders

use_cases.title: Outbound signal enrichment
use_cases.description: Outbound agencies and Clay power-users layer Letterdrop's signals on top of enrichment workflows.

use_cases.title: Founder-led GTM
use_cases.description: Early-stage startups use Letterdrop to activate founders online and generate inbound demos.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Letterdrop?
paragraphs: Letterdrop is a competitor-monitoring and pipeline-intelligence platform for B2B sales teams. It identifies specific contacts who are actively starting sales cycles with a company's chosen competitors, rather than showing account-level research interest.

Signals are delivered daily into Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Apollo, Outreach, Clay, or a spreadsheet, each with a confidence score and a recommended outreach play, and the platform also connects to ChatGPT and Claude via MCP.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Letterdrop is for
paragraphs: It's built for sales-led companies with at least 50 employees and a repeatable outbound motion, including VPs of Sales, demand gen leaders, outbound agencies, and Clay power-users.

enrichment.short_description: An API-first headless CMS with a free plan, official framework SDKs, and built-in SEO and AI content-assist tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ButterCMS is an API-first headless CMS with a free tier, SDKs for React/Next.js/Angular/Django/Laravel, and built-in SEO and AI tools.

features.title: Content modeling
features.description: Structure content once and reuse it across websites, ecommerce, and mobile apps.

features.title: Developer tooling
features.description: APIs, SDKs, and webhooks with official integrations for Angular, Django, Laravel, Next.js, and React.

features.title: Workflow and collaboration
features.description: User roles and approval flows for streamlining content publishing across a team.

features.title: Multisite and multi-language support
features.description: Manage multiple brands, regions, and languages from a single CMS instance.

features.title: Built-in SEO fields
features.description: SEO metadata and fast-loading HTML delivery are built into content pages.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: AI-powered content blocks and suggestions to speed up content creation.

features.title: Media and asset management
features.description: Centralized media library with drag-and-drop uploads and edits.

pros: Free plan with no credit card required, plus a 14-day free trial on every paid plan
Official SDKs/integrations for React, Vue, Next.js, Angular, Django, and Laravel
Published, self-serve pricing through the Professional tier
Includes an AI Assistant and built-in SEO metadata on every plan
cons: Usage is tightly metered — API calls, bandwidth, pages, blog posts, and assets all have plan-specific caps
Multi-locale support isn't available until the Professional plan
Enterprise features (custom limits, dedicated support) require contacting sales for custom pricing

faqs.question: Do I need a credit card for the Free plan?
faqs.answer: No, the Free plan gives full access to core features with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What's included in the Free plan?
faqs.answer: Unlimited users and components, 50K API calls/month, 100GB bandwidth/month, up to 5 pages, 50 blog posts, 50 collections, and 500 assets.

faqs.question: Does ButterCMS offer a free trial on paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, every paid plan includes a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Which frameworks does ButterCMS officially support?
faqs.answer: Angular, Django, Laravel, Next.js, and React have official integrations and quickstart guides.

use_cases.title: Adding a CMS layer to an app
use_cases.description: Developers add ButterCMS as a headless content layer to an existing product using its APIs and SDKs.

use_cases.title: Marketing-led publishing
use_cases.description: Marketers publish and update landing pages and blog content without filing developer tickets.

use_cases.title: Multi-brand content management
use_cases.description: Teams manage content across multiple sites, brands, or languages from one CMS.

features: Custom API calls, bandwidth, pages, collections, assets, roles, and locales

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ButterCMS?
paragraphs: ButterCMS is an API-first headless CMS designed to let marketers publish content quickly while developers integrate it into React, Vue, Next.js, Angular, Django, or Laravel projects using official SDKs.
It includes workflow tools (roles and approval flows), multisite/multi-language support, built-in SEO fields, and an AI Assistant for content creation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ButterCMS is for
paragraphs: It suits developers who want a hosted, API-driven CMS without building one from scratch, and marketers who need to publish and update content without waiting on engineering.

enrichment.short_description: A composable digital experience platform combining a headless CMS with AI-driven personalization, analytics, and automation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Contentful is a composable digital experience platform combining a headless CMS with AI-driven personalization and analytics.

features.title: Composable content platform
features.description: Delivers content across web, apps, and other channels from a single source.

features.title: Personalization
features.description: Tools for creating and running personalized, tailored experiences for different audience segments.

features.title: AI Actions
features.description: Automates and streamlines content workflows using AI.

features.title: Studio
features.description: Visually assemble digital experiences at scale.

features.title: Palmata
features.description: A newer Contentful product focused on understanding and improving how an organization is represented in AI discovery/search.

features.title: Marketplace
features.description: Apps and integrations for extending the platform's capabilities.

features.title: Free plan quotas
features.description: The Free plan includes 100K API calls/month and 50GB asset bandwidth/month, with no overages allowed.

pros: Broader platform than a basic CMS, adding personalization, analytics, and AI-driven content automation
Published customer case studies show measurable results (e.g. faster release cycles, higher conversion rates)
Free plan available with a meaningful included quota (100K API calls, 50GB bandwidth/month)
Marketplace of apps and integrations for extending the platform
cons: Paid tier (Lite/Enterprise) pricing isn't published publicly and requires contacting sales
Free plan is hard-capped with no overages allowed on API calls or bandwidth
Broader platform scope (personalization, analytics, AI Actions) adds more surface area than a simple headless CMS

faqs.question: Does Contentful have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Free plan is available and includes a Starter Space with published usage limits.

faqs.question: Is pricing public for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Only the Free plan has published self-serve limits; Lite and Enterprise plans require contacting sales.

faqs.question: What is Palmata?
faqs.answer: Palmata is Contentful's newer product for understanding and improving how an organization is represented in AI discovery and search.

faqs.question: Does Contentful support personalization?
faqs.answer: Yes, Contentful Personalization lets teams create tailored experiences and run experimentation.

use_cases.title: Composable digital experience platform
use_cases.description: Enterprises use Contentful to unify content delivery across web, personalization, and analytics.

use_cases.title: Headless content delivery
use_cases.description: Developers use Contentful's APIs to power custom front-ends.

use_cases.title: Personalization and experimentation
use_cases.description: Growth and marketing teams run audience segmentation and experiments on content.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Contentful?
paragraphs: Contentful is a composable digital experience platform that combines a headless CMS with personalization, AI-driven content automation (AI Actions), and analytics tools like Studio.
It also offers Palmata, a newer product focused on how organizations are represented in AI-powered discovery and search.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Contentful is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at enterprise and mid-market teams that need to manage content across many channels and want personalization and analytics built into the same platform, alongside developers building headless front-ends.

enrichment.short_description: A headless CMS with a visual, component-based editor, an API-first architecture, and a free Starter plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Storyblok is a headless CMS with a visual, component-based editor, API-first delivery, and a free Starter plan for small projects.

features.title: Visual editor with Global Components
features.description: Component-based content blocks that developers build once and marketers reuse and rearrange visually.

features.title: API-first delivery
features.description: GraphQL API, REST API, and Management APIs, plus an MCP Server for AI agent access.

features.title: Multi-locale content
features.description: Locale support included from the free Starter plan, with more locales available on higher tiers.

features.title: AI credits
features.description: Included AI credits (25k on the Starter plan) power AI-assisted content features.

features.title: Seat-based collaboration
features.description: Plans include a set number of user seats, with additional seats available for a per-seat fee.

features.title: ISO 27001 certified security
features.description: Certified security practices with up to 99.99% uptime SLA on enterprise tiers.

features.title: Native integrations
features.description: Includes integrations such as Shopify on the Growth plan and above.

pros: Free Starter plan plus a 45-day free trial of the Growth Plus plan for new projects
Transparent, published self-serve pricing through the Growth and Growth Plus tiers
API-first architecture with a visual editor, suited to both developers and marketers
ISO 27001 certified with high uptime SLAs on higher tiers
cons: Team size, locales, traffic, and API calls are all metered, with extra cost on every tier
Enterprise features (custom roles, SSO, SCIM, 99.9% SLA) require a custom Premium/Elite quote
Free Starter plan is limited to 1 space and 2 locales

faqs.question: Does Storyblok have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free and includes 1 space, 1 user seat, and 100GB traffic per month.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 45-day free trial of the Growth Plus plan is offered before committing to a paid plan.

faqs.question: When was Storyblok founded?
faqs.answer: Storyblok was started in 2017 in Austria by founders Dominik and Alexander.

faqs.question: Does Storyblok support AI or agent workflows?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers an MCP Server and includes AI credits for AI-assisted content features.

use_cases.title: Visual content editing
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use the visual editor and Global Components to build and rearrange pages without code.

use_cases.title: Headless site development
use_cases.description: Developers build front-ends with Next.js, Nuxt, or Astro against Storyblok's APIs.

use_cases.title: Multi-brand enterprise content
use_cases.description: Enterprises manage content across multiple brands, regions, and locales.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Storyblok?
paragraphs: Storyblok is a headless CMS founded in 2017 in Austria, built around a visual editor and reusable component blocks ('Global Components') on top of an API-first architecture (GraphQL, REST, and Management APIs).
It offers a free Starter plan, a self-serve Growth and Growth Plus tier with published pricing, and custom Premium/Elite tiers for enterprise needs like SSO and custom SLAs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Storyblok is for
paragraphs: It's built for teams where both developers and marketers need to work in the same CMS — developers building reusable components, marketers assembling pages visually without code.

enrichment.short_description: A headless CMS and page builder with AI translation and an MCP integration letting ChatGPT or Claude update pages via prompt.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Prismic is a headless CMS and page builder with reusable slices, AI translation, and an MCP integration for ChatGPT and Claude.

features.title: Visual Page Builder
features.description: A headless CMS where pages are assembled from reusable, brand-approved sections called slices.

features.title: Slice Machine
features.description: A local developer tool for coding and customizing slices within a normal development workflow.

features.title: AI translation
features.description: Creates and manages multilingual content while keeping each locale aligned to a master page structure.

features.title: AI landing page builder
features.description: A separately priced add-on that uses AI to scale creation of on-brand landing pages.

features.title: MCP integration with ChatGPT and Claude
features.description: AI assistants can query and edit pages across the site directly through a single prompt.

features.title: Built-in SEO/GEO schema support
features.description: Pages include schema support aimed at visibility in AI search and Google Search.

features.title: Migration API
features.description: Included even on the Free plan for moving content into or out of Prismic.

pros: Free plan includes 4M API calls/month and unlimited documents, types, and assets
Self-serve pricing is fully published from Free through the Platinum tier
Founded in 2013 with 10,000+ websites built on the platform
Native MCP integration lets ChatGPT and Claude make prompt-driven edits across pages
cons: User seats are limited on lower tiers — 1 user on Free, 3 on Starter
The AI landing page builder is a separate paid add-on, not included in base plans
Enterprise features like SSO and a dedicated Customer Success Manager require a custom quote

faqs.question: Is Prismic free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 1 user, 4 million API calls/month, and 100GB of CDN bandwidth/month at no cost.

faqs.question: How is Prismic priced?
faqs.answer: Per repository, billed annually, ranging from Free up to Platinum at $675/month, with Enterprise available on request.

faqs.question: Where is Prismic based?
faqs.answer: Prismic's headquarters is in Paris, France.

faqs.question: Does Prismic support AI-assisted editing?
faqs.answer: Yes, including AI translation and prompt-driven page updates through its MCP integration with ChatGPT and Claude.

use_cases.title: SEO and ABM landing pages
use_cases.description: Marketing teams build landing pages for SEO and account-based marketing at scale using slices.

use_cases.title: Component-based site development
use_cases.description: Developers build reusable page sections with Next.js or Nuxt using Slice Machine.

use_cases.title: Multi-client site management
use_cases.description: Agencies manage multiple client websites from a single platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Prismic?
paragraphs: Prismic, founded in 2013 in Paris, is a headless CMS and page builder built around reusable content sections called slices, developed locally with a tool called Slice Machine.
It has since added AI features including AI translation, an AI landing page add-on, and an MCP integration that lets ChatGPT and Claude query and edit pages directly through a prompt.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Prismic is for
paragraphs: It's used by marketing teams building SEO and ABM landing pages, developers building reusable page sections, and agencies managing multiple client sites on one platform.

enrichment.short_description: A headless CMS with a GraphQL API, structured text editor, built-in localization, and a free-forever plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: DatoCMS is a headless CMS with a GraphQL API, structured text editor, built-in localization, and a free-forever plan.

features.title: GraphQL Content Delivery API
features.description: Content is delivered through a GraphQL-based Content Delivery API alongside a REST Content Management API.

features.title: Structured Text editor
features.description: A block-based rich text editor for structured, reusable content.

features.title: Built-in localization
features.description: Multi-locale content management is included, with 5 locales on both the Free and Professional plans.

features.title: Modular content modeling
features.description: Content is built from reusable, component-based models for dynamic page building.

features.title: Visual Editing
features.description: Content can be edited directly on the page rather than hunting through records.

features.title: Plugin ecosystem
features.description: Over 200 plugins are available to expand and customize the CMS.

features.title: Cache Tags
features.description: Fine-grained cache invalidation for high-performance content delivery.

pros: Free-forever plan with no credit card required, and a no-signup live demo
Transparent published pricing for both Free and Professional tiers, with clear add-on pricing for overages
Large plugin marketplace (200+) and an active Slack developer community (3,000+ members)
Structured Text and modular content modeling suited to complex, multi-format sites
cons: Free plan is capped at 300 records, 10GB traffic/month, and 1 collaborator, with no overages allowed
Higher usage (extra projects, records, bandwidth, API calls) is billed as recurring add-ons on top of the Professional price
Community-based support only on the Free plan; faster email support requires the paid Professional tier

faqs.question: Is DatoCMS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a free-forever plan with 2 editors, 300 records, and 100k API calls/month, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much is the paid plan?
faqs.answer: The Professional plan starts at €149/month billed annually, or €199/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: What happens if I exceed the Free plan's limits?
faqs.answer: The service stops responding as expected, since no overages are allowed on the Free plan.

faqs.question: Does DatoCMS support multiple languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, localization is built in, with 5 locales included on both the Free and Professional plans and additional locales available as an add-on.

use_cases.title: Agency client sites
use_cases.description: Digital agencies build multilingual client sites and get a discount through DatoCMS's Agency Partner Program.

use_cases.title: Modern front-end development
use_cases.description: Developers build with Next.js, Nuxt, Astro, or SvelteKit against DatoCMS's GraphQL API.

use_cases.title: Structured content management
use_cases.description: Content teams manage modular, structured content across a website using the Structured Text editor.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is DatoCMS?
paragraphs: DatoCMS is a headless CMS built around a GraphQL Content Delivery API, a Structured Text editor, and built-in localization, aimed at both developers and content teams.

It offers a free-forever plan with published limits (2 editors, 300 records, 100k API calls/month) and a Professional plan starting at €149/month billed annually, with clearly priced add-ons for extra usage.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who DatoCMS is for
paragraphs: It's used by developers building with modern frameworks like Next.js, Nuxt, and Astro, as well as digital agencies and content teams managing structured, multilingual content.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source headless CMS that auto-generates REST and GraphQL APIs from customizable content types.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Strapi is an open-source headless CMS for building customizable REST and GraphQL content APIs.

features.title: Content Type Builder
features.description: No-code interface for creating and managing content models.

features.title: Automatic API generation
features.description: Generates REST and GraphQL APIs directly from content models.

features.title: Components & Dynamic Zones
features.description: Reusable content blocks editors can rearrange within page structures.

features.title: Custom fields
features.description: Extensible field types for tailored data structures.

features.title: Node.js customization
features.description: Full Node.js app supports custom controllers, services, and middleware.

features.title: Strapi MCP
features.description: Model Context Protocol support lets AI agents manage Strapi content.

features.title: Community marketplace
features.description: Reviewed plugins and integrations built by the community.

pros: MIT-licensed and free to self-host with no feature caps on the core product.
Automatically generates REST and GraphQL APIs from content models.
Large open-source community (72.7k+ GitHub stars).
Enterprise tier adds SSO, audit logs, and review workflows.
Native MCP support lets AI agents interact with content.
cons: SSO, audit logs, and review workflows require paid Enterprise-tier plans, not the free self-hosted core.
Strapi Cloud managed hosting starts at $35/month per project.
Additional cloud environments cost extra ($60-$300/month depending on plan).
Self-hosting requires developer resources to deploy and maintain.

faqs.question: Is Strapi free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Strapi CMS is MIT-licensed and free to self-host; Strapi Cloud managed hosting starts at $35/month per project.

faqs.question: Does Strapi support GraphQL?
faqs.answer: Yes, Strapi automatically generates both REST and GraphQL APIs from your content models.

faqs.question: What is Strapi Cloud?
faqs.answer: A managed hosting option with built-in Postgres, CDN, and email, starting at $35/month for the Starter plan.

faqs.question: Can AI agents work with Strapi?
faqs.answer: Yes, Strapi MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets AI agents manage content directly.

use_cases.title: Custom API-driven websites
use_cases.description: Developers build content-backed APIs for React, Vue, Next.js, and other frontends.

use_cases.title: Enterprise content platforms
use_cases.description: Organizations needing SSO, audit logs, and review workflows on Strapi Enterprise.

use_cases.title: Multi-framework content delivery
use_cases.description: Teams shipping content to web, mobile, and native apps from one API.

features: All Business features with customizable limits

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Strapi?
paragraphs: Strapi is an open-source, MIT-licensed headless CMS that lets developers define content types and automatically generates REST and GraphQL APIs from them. It can be self-hosted for free or run on Strapi Cloud, a managed hosting option starting at $35/month.
Because it's a real Node.js application, teams can extend Strapi with custom controllers, services, and middleware, and reshape the admin panel to fit their workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Strapi is for
paragraphs: Strapi suits development teams that want full control over their content API and infrastructure, from solo developers self-hosting for free to enterprises needing SSO and audit logs on paid tiers.

enrichment.short_description: Headless CMS storing structured content as JSON, with a real-time Studio editor and an AI-powered Content Agent.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sanity is a headless CMS with structured content, real-time editing, and an AI-powered Content Agent.

features.title: Structured content
features.description: Stores content as JSON documents with schemas configured in Studio.

features.title: Sanity Studio
features.description: Customizable, multiplayer content editing interface.

features.title: Content Agent
features.description: AI automation for transforming, auditing, and preparing content changes.

features.title: Visual Editing
features.description: Preview and edit content in context across frameworks like Next.js and Astro.

features.title: Content Releases
features.description: Precision publishing and scheduling for content changes.

features.title: GROQ query language
features.description: Custom query syntax for retrieving structured content.

features.title: Real-time APIs
features.description: Live content and listening APIs for dynamic updates.

pros: Free plan includes 20 seats and 10k documents with no recurring charge.
Structured, JSON-based content model works across web, mobile, and agentic apps.
Built-in Content Agent automates content transformation and auditing.
Supports major frameworks: Next.js, Astro, SvelteKit, Nuxt.js, React Router/Remix.
SOC 2, GDPR, and CCPA compliant.
cons: Growth plan pricing is per seat ($15/seat/month), which scales with team size.
SSO/SAML, Media Library, and custom retention are Enterprise-only, quote-based features.
Dedicated support ($799/mo) and extra datasets ($999/dataset/mo) are paid add-ons on top of Growth.
Free plan datasets are public only; private datasets require the Growth plan.

faqs.question: Does Sanity have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 20 seats, 2 public datasets, 10k documents, and 100GB of assets and bandwidth.

faqs.question: What is GROQ?
faqs.answer: Sanity's custom query language for retrieving structured content from the platform.

faqs.question: What does Content Agent do?
faqs.answer: It's an AI-powered feature for transforming source material, auditing content, and preparing changes.

faqs.question: How much does Sanity cost?
faqs.answer: Free, then Growth at $15/seat/month (up to 50 seats), or custom Enterprise pricing.

use_cases.title: Structured, reusable content
use_cases.description: Teams model content once and reuse it across web, mobile, and other channels.

use_cases.title: Multi-channel content delivery
use_cases.description: Developers build apps on Next.js, Astro, SvelteKit, and other frameworks using Sanity's APIs.

use_cases.title: Enterprise governance
use_cases.description: Organizations needing SSO, audit trails, and custom retention on the Enterprise plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sanity?
paragraphs: Sanity is a headless CMS that stores content as structured JSON documents, edited through Sanity Studio, a customizable, real-time collaborative interface. It includes an AI-powered Content Agent for transforming, auditing, and preparing content changes.
The platform offers a free plan for up to 20 seats, a per-seat Growth plan starting at $15/seat/month, and custom Enterprise pricing for advanced governance features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sanity is for
paragraphs: Sanity fits content and development teams that need structured, reusable content across multiple channels, from small teams on the free plan to enterprises requiring SSO and custom compliance controls.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source headless CMS that connects to any existing database and auto-generates REST and GraphQL APIs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Directus is an open-source headless CMS that turns any database into a REST and GraphQL API.

features.title: Instant API generation
features.description: Automatic REST and GraphQL API creation from an existing database schema.

features.title: No-code Studio
features.description: Visual interface for content editing and data management.

features.title: Digital asset manager
features.description: File upload, organization, and on-demand image transformation.

features.title: Real-time updates
features.description: WebSocket support for live data subscriptions.

features.title: MCP Server
features.description: Native integration with Claude Desktop, Cursor, ChatGPT, and other MCP-compatible tools.

features.title: Built-in AI Assistant
features.description: Conversational AI for content creation, translation, and record management.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Governance applied to both human users and AI agents.

pros: Free, self-hosted Core plan with no artificial feature caps.
Connects directly to an existing database instead of requiring data migration.
Supports 15+ frontend frameworks including Next.js, React, Vue/Nuxt, and Flutter.
Native MCP server for AI assistants like Claude Desktop and Cursor.
SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliant.
Open Innovation Grant offers free/discounted access for small orgs and nonprofits.
cons: Team plan starts at $499/month (annual) or $599/month (monthly) for SSO-enabled seats.
Custom LLM support and AI translations are reserved for the Enterprise tier.
Cloud hosting for the free Core plan costs an additional $99/month.
Extra seats on the Team plan cost $50/seat/month.

faqs.question: Is Directus free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Core plan is free to self-host with no credit card required; Directus Cloud hosting is a $99/month add-on.

faqs.question: Do I need to migrate my database to use Directus?
faqs.answer: No, Directus connects to your existing database and generates APIs on top of it.

faqs.question: Does Directus support AI agents?
faqs.answer: Yes, via a native MCP server plus a built-in AI Assistant for content and translation tasks.

faqs.question: What is the Open Innovation Grant?
faqs.answer: Free full-featured access for organizations under $5M annual revenue and fewer than 50 employees.

use_cases.title: Turning legacy databases into APIs
use_cases.description: Backend teams expose existing databases as REST/GraphQL APIs without migrating data.

use_cases.title: Multi-framework content delivery
use_cases.description: Full-stack teams ship content to 15+ supported frontend frameworks.

use_cases.title: Nonprofits and small orgs
use_cases.description: Organizations under $5M revenue can access Directus free via the Open Innovation Grant.

features: Full feature access, no artificial caps
For orgs under $5M revenue and <50 employees

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Directus?
paragraphs: Directus is an open-source backend and headless CMS that connects directly to an existing database and automatically generates REST and GraphQL APIs along with a no-code admin app called Studio.
It's free to self-host as the Core plan, with paid Team ($499-599/month) and custom Enterprise tiers adding SSO, more Flows, and dedicated support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Directus is for
paragraphs: Directus suits backend developers who want to expose an existing database as an API without migrating data, and organizations that want built-in AI assistance and MCP support for content management.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, code-first headless CMS and backend built specifically for Next.js, with an auto-generated admin panel.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Payload is an open-source, code-first headless CMS and backend built specifically for Next.js.

features.title: Code-first schema
features.description: Content structure is defined directly in code rather than through a UI.

features.title: Auto-generated admin panel
features.description: Admin interface is generated automatically from the schema definition.

features.title: Visual editing
features.description: Build and preview how content will appear in real time.

features.title: RAG & vector embeddings
features.description: Automatic content preparation for AI/RAG applications with no manual setup.

features.title: Multi-tenancy & white-labeling
features.description: Built-in support for multi-tenant and white-label deployments.

features.title: Localization & access control
features.description: Native localization and configurable access control rules.

features.title: Built-in authentication
features.description: Auth features included out of the box.

pros: MIT-licensed and free to self-host with no cost to deploy.
Deep native integration with Next.js rather than a bolt-on integration.
Automatic vector embeddings and RAG support for AI applications with zero manual setup.
Works with MongoDB, Postgres, or SQLite.
Instant-deploy templates available for Vercel and Cloudflare.
cons: No public self-serve pricing beyond free self-hosting; Enterprise plan pricing requires a sales demo.
Managed hosting pricing (Vercel/Cloudflare templates) is not disclosed on Payload's own site.
Built specifically for Next.js, so it isn't framework-agnostic like some other headless CMS options.
Payload was acquired by Figma, which could shift its independent product direction over time.

faqs.question: Is Payload free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Payload is MIT-licensed and free to self-host; Enterprise support and hosting have separate, undisclosed pricing.

faqs.question: Is Payload built specifically for Next.js?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's designed to run natively inside Next.js applications without separate infrastructure.

faqs.question: Who owns Payload?
faqs.answer: Payload is now part of Figma, as announced on Figma's blog, and remains open source.

faqs.question: Does Payload support AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes automatic vector embedding and RAG preparation for AI applications.

use_cases.title: Embedded CMS for Next.js apps
use_cases.description: Developers add a full CMS and admin panel directly inside a Next.js codebase.

use_cases.title: AI-ready content pipelines
use_cases.description: Teams use built-in RAG and vector embedding support to feed content into AI applications.

use_cases.title: Multi-tenant platforms
use_cases.description: Organizations build white-labeled, multi-tenant products on Payload's built-in support.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Payload CMS?
paragraphs: Payload is an open-source, code-first headless CMS and backend built specifically for Next.js, with an admin panel that's auto-generated from your schema. It's MIT-licensed and free to self-host on MongoDB, Postgres, or SQLite.
Payload is now part of Figma, according to Figma's own announcement, though it remains open source and continues to be actively developed.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Payload CMS is for
paragraphs: Payload suits Next.js developers who want a CMS and backend embedded directly in their codebase, plus teams building AI-ready content pipelines that need built-in RAG and vector embedding support.

enrichment.short_description: GraphQL-native headless CMS for managing structured, relational content across multiple channels at enterprise scale.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hygraph is a GraphQL-native headless CMS for structured, relational content at enterprise scale.

features.title: Content federation
features.description: GraphQL-native architecture that combines multiple data sources without duplication.

features.title: Smart Edge Cache
features.description: Delivers a 3-5x latency improvement on read-only endpoints.

features.title: Granular RBAC & SSO
features.description: Role-based access control with OIDC/LDAP/SAML single sign-on.

features.title: Audit logs & encryption
features.description: Activity logging plus encryption in transit and at rest.

features.title: Multi-language localization
features.description: Built-in support for managing content across multiple locales.

features.title: MCP Server API
features.description: API layer for connecting AI assistants to Hygraph content.

pros: Free Hobby plan includes 3 seats and unlimited asset storage.
GraphQL-native architecture supports federating multiple content sources.
SOC 2 Type 2 (since 2022) and ISO 27001 certified.
MCP Server API allows AI assistants to connect to content.
GDPR and German data-protection (BDSG/TMG) compliant.
cons: Growth plan jumps to $199/month from the free Hobby tier, with no mid-tier option shown.
SSO, multitenancy, and dedicated infrastructure require custom Enterprise pricing.
Hobby plan is capped at 1,000 entries and 500,000 API calls per month.
Founding year and headquarters are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: Is Hygraph free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Hobby plan is free forever, with 3 seats and unlimited asset storage up to certain usage limits.

faqs.question: Does Hygraph support content federation?
faqs.answer: Yes, its GraphQL-native architecture can combine multiple data sources without duplicating data.

faqs.question: Is Hygraph SOC 2 certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, it has been SOC 2 Type 2 certified since August 2022, and is also ISO 27001 certified.

faqs.question: Can AI assistants connect to Hygraph?
faqs.answer: Yes, via the MCP Server API designed for AI assistant integration.

use_cases.title: Federating multiple content sources
use_cases.description: Enterprise teams combine data from several systems into one GraphQL API.

use_cases.title: Multi-language content delivery
use_cases.description: Global marketing teams manage localized content across many locales.

use_cases.title: Regulated industries
use_cases.description: Companies in healthcare, fintech, and automotive rely on SOC 2/ISO 27001 compliance.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hygraph?
paragraphs: Hygraph is a GraphQL-native headless CMS built for managing structured, relational content and federating it from multiple data sources into a single API. It offers a free Hobby plan, a Growth plan starting at $199/month, and custom Enterprise pricing.
Security features include SOC 2 Type 2 and ISO 27001 certification, along with SSO and granular RBAC on higher tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hygraph is for
paragraphs: Hygraph fits enterprises and high-growth companies that need to combine content from multiple systems via GraphQL federation, particularly in regulated industries needing SOC 2/ISO 27001 compliance.

enrichment.short_description: Agentic customer service platform for ecommerce brands that unifies channels and resolves routine inquiries with its Mim AI agent.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Dixa is an agentic customer service platform that unifies channels and automates support with AI.

features.title: Mim AI Agent
features.description: Autonomously resolves routine inquiries like refunds, tracking, and order changes across chat, email, and WhatsApp.

features.title: Team Hub
features.description: Unified workspace showing orders, conversation history, loyalty status, and AI-suggested replies.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Visual, no-code routing and automation builder.

features.title: Omnichannel support
features.description: Integrates phone, email, chat, social media, and WhatsApp in one platform.

features.title: Dixa Discover
features.description: Automatic conversation scoring and trend analysis for performance and QA.

features.title: AI Co-Pilot
features.description: Assists agents with response suggestions, knowledge surfacing, and translation.

pros: Unifies phone, email, chat, social, and WhatsApp conversations in a single workspace.
Mim AI Agent autonomously handles routine inquiries across multiple channels.
No-code visual workflow and routing automation builder.
Used by 850+ brands across 42 countries.
35+ native integrations included from the Growth plan up.
cons: No free plan; the entry-level Growth plan costs €89/agent/month.
Mim AI Agent usage is billed separately at €0.35 per conversation on top of seat fees.
SSO, advanced AI intent detection, and multi-org support are reserved for the top Prime tier at €179/agent/month.
Founding year and headquarters are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: Does Dixa offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, published plans start at €89/agent/month for the Growth tier.

faqs.question: What is Mim?
faqs.answer: Dixa's AI agent that autonomously resolves routine customer inquiries across chat, email, and WhatsApp.

faqs.question: How is Mim AI Agent usage billed?
faqs.answer: Separately from seat fees, at €0.35 per AI-handled conversation.

faqs.question: Which channels does Dixa support?
faqs.answer: Phone, email, chat, social media, and WhatsApp, unified in one Team Hub.

use_cases.title: Scaling ecommerce support without headcount growth
use_cases.description: Brands use Mim AI Agent to absorb routine inquiries as order volume grows.

use_cases.title: Enterprise support operations
use_cases.description: Larger teams use the Prime plan for SSO, multi-org support, and advanced routing.

features: Contact center (phone, email, forms, chat, social)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Dixa?
paragraphs: Dixa is a customer service platform for ecommerce brands that unifies phone, email, chat, social, and WhatsApp conversations into one Team Hub, and uses its Mim AI Agent to autonomously resolve routine inquiries such as refunds and order tracking.
Plans start at €89/agent/month for Growth, scaling up to €179/agent/month for Prime, with Mim AI Agent usage billed separately at €0.35 per conversation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Dixa is for
paragraphs: Dixa is built for ecommerce support teams that want to automate routine inquiries while keeping conversations unified across channels, from mid-market brands on Growth to enterprises needing SSO and multi-org support on Prime.

enrichment.short_description: Customer experience AI platform that unifies support conversations across channels around a single customer timeline.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gladly is a customer experience AI platform unifying support conversations across every channel.

features.title: Multi-channel support
features.description: Voice, chat, SMS, email, and social media unified under one conversation history.

features.title: AI agent
features.description: Resolves conversations independently or hands off to human agents.

features.title: Guides
features.description: Plain-English controls that let business users configure brand voice and AI behavior without engineering.

features.title: Product catalog integration
features.description: Brings a company's full product catalog into Gladly.

features.title: Customer intelligence
features.description: Surfaces agent context and customer history for more personalized conversations.

pros: Organizes support around a single customer timeline instead of siloed tickets per channel.
Non-technical "Guides" let business users configure AI behavior without engineering help.
Publicly names enterprise customers including Ulta Beauty, Nordstrom, and Crate & Barrel.
Combines voice, chat, SMS, email, and social in one unified conversation view.
cons: No published self-serve pricing; sign-up requires a demo or sales contact.
No listed free trial or free tier on the site.
Performance figures (e.g. 76% AI resolution, $510M+ saved) are Gladly's own reported case-study claims rather than independently audited results.
Founding year and headquarters are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: How much does Gladly cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing isn't published; it requires requesting a demo or contacting sales.

faqs.question: What is Gladly?
faqs.answer: A customer experience AI platform that unifies conversations around a single customer timeline across channels.

faqs.question: What channels does Gladly support?
faqs.answer: Voice, chat, SMS, email, and social media.

faqs.question: Who uses Gladly?
faqs.answer: Named customers include Ulta Beauty, Nordstrom, TUMI, UGG, and Crate & Barrel.

use_cases.title: Unifying customer conversations across channels
use_cases.description: Retail brands replace channel-siloed tickets with one continuous customer timeline.

use_cases.title: Non-technical AI configuration
use_cases.description: Business users adjust AI behavior and brand voice through plain-English Guides.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gladly?
paragraphs: Gladly is a customer experience AI platform that organizes support around a single, continuous customer timeline rather than per-channel tickets, spanning voice, chat, SMS, email, and social media.

Its AI agent can resolve conversations independently or collaborate with human agents, and business users can configure AI behavior through plain-English "Guides" without engineering support. Pricing is not published and requires contacting Gladly directly.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gladly is for
paragraphs: Gladly is aimed at retail and consumer brands that want a relationship-focused, single-timeline view of each customer across every support channel, as reflected in its named customers like Ulta Beauty and Nordstrom.

enrichment.short_description: AI-native helpdesk for ecommerce that autonomously resolves support tickets and unifies channels in one inbox.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Richpanel is an AI-native helpdesk that automatically resolves ecommerce customer support tickets.

features.title: AI Resolution
features.description: Autonomously handles support tickets, guaranteeing 50% resolution within the first 30 days.

features.title: Omnichannel inbox
features.description: Unifies email, chat, Instagram, WhatsApp, SMS, and voice in one inbox.

features.title: Self-service portal
features.description: Lets customers track orders, process returns, and manage subscriptions independently.

features.title: AI Copilot
features.description: Drafts replies and executes actions for human agent review.

features.title: Social media moderation
features.description: AI handles comments and DMs across social channels.

features.title: Multi-brand management
features.description: Operate multiple brands from a single workspace.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Tracks revenue, team performance, CSAT, and self-service metrics.

pros: Usage-based AI pricing (~$0.20/conversation) instead of flat per-resolution fees.
Guarantees 50% of tickets resolved by AI within 30 days or a refund.
Self-service portal for orders, returns, and subscriptions reduces incoming ticket volume.
SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, and GDPR compliant.
40+ integrations including Shopify, Recharge, Loop Returns, and AfterShip.
cons: No published flat/free tier — cost is built from $99/seat/month plus AI usage fees, with a $200/month minimum.
Cost scales with AI conversation volume, which can be unpredictable as support volume grows.
Exact pricing requires using an interactive calculator rather than fixed published tiers.
Founding year and headquarters are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: How is Richpanel priced?
faqs.answer: $99/seat/month for helpdesk seats plus roughly $0.20 per AI-handled conversation, with a $200/month minimum.

faqs.question: Does Richpanel offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: The site describes a risk-free trial period backed by its AI resolution money-back guarantee.

faqs.question: What is the AI resolution guarantee?
faqs.answer: Richpanel guarantees 50% of tickets resolved by AI within 30 days, or a refund.

faqs.question: Which channels does Richpanel support?
faqs.answer: Email, chat, Instagram, WhatsApp, SMS, and voice, all in one inbox.

use_cases.title: Cutting support costs with AI
use_cases.description: Ecommerce brands offload routine tickets to AI resolution instead of hiring proportionally.

use_cases.title: Multi-brand support operations
use_cases.description: Companies running several storefronts manage support for all of them from one workspace.

features: AI conversations billed on actual token use

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Richpanel?
paragraphs: Richpanel is an AI-native helpdesk built for ecommerce brands, combining a shared inbox with an AI Resolution engine that autonomously handles support tickets across email, chat, Instagram, WhatsApp, SMS, and voice.

Pricing is usage-based: $99/agent/month for helpdesk seats plus roughly $0.20 per AI-handled conversation, with a $200/month minimum and a guarantee that AI resolves at least 50% of tickets within 30 days.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Richpanel is for
paragraphs: Richpanel is aimed at ecommerce brands that want to reduce support headcount growth by automating routine tickets while giving customers a self-service portal for orders, returns, and subscriptions.

enrichment.short_description: Customer service platform combining live chat, email, social channels, and an AI agent in one inbox for ecommerce.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Re:amaze is a helpdesk platform combining live chat, email, social channels, and an AI agent.

features.title: Intelligent Inbox
features.description: Consolidates messages from multiple channels with team collaboration tools.

features.title: AI Agent (Beta)
features.description: 24/7 automated support assistant trainable on business details.

features.title: Cues
features.description: Proactive customer engagement through trigger-based messages.

features.title: Peek
features.description: Real-time co-browsing to monitor customer behavior on-site.

features.title: AI-powered knowledge base
features.description: FAQ center that also trains the AI Agent.

features.title: Workflows
features.description: Automation for common support tasks and triggers.

features.title: Mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps with push notifications.

pros: Flat-rate Starter plan ($59/month) gives unlimited team members up to 500 conversations/month.
14-day free trial with no credit card required, including all Plus-plan features.
Built-in co-browsing (Peek) and proactive messaging (Cues) beyond standard helpdesk features.
Native iOS and Android mobile apps.
Month-to-month billing with no contracts or cancellation fees.
cons: No permanent free plan, only a 14-day trial.
AI Agent is labeled "Beta."
SMS/voice channels, custom domain, and screensharing require the Pro or Plus plans ($49-$69/user/month).
Flat-rate Starter plan is capped at 500 responded conversations per month.

faqs.question: Does Re:amaze have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with all Plus-plan features and no credit card required.

faqs.question: Where is Re:amaze based?
faqs.answer: San Jose, California, United States.

faqs.question: What is the Starter plan?
faqs.answer: A $59/month flat rate for unlimited team members, capped at 500 responded conversations per month.

faqs.question: Does Re:amaze support SMS and voice?
faqs.answer: Yes, SMS and voice channels are available starting on the Pro plan.

use_cases.title: Live chat plus email in one inbox
use_cases.description: Ecommerce stores manage all customer messages from a single Intelligent Inbox.

use_cases.title: Flat-rate support for small teams
use_cases.description: Teams on a budget use the Starter plan for unlimited seats at a fixed monthly cost.

use_cases.title: Proactive customer engagement
use_cases.description: Brands use Cues and Peek to engage and observe site visitors in real time.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Re:amaze?
paragraphs: Re:amaze, based in San Jose, California, is a customer service platform that consolidates email, live chat, social media, SMS, VoIP, and video calls into one Intelligent Inbox for ecommerce and online businesses.
Plans start at $29/user/month (Basic) or a $59/month flat rate (Starter, unlimited team members up to 500 conversations), with a 14-day free trial available on all plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Re:amaze is for
paragraphs: Re:amaze suits ecommerce and online businesses that want live chat, email, and social support unified in one inbox, from small teams on the flat-rate Starter plan to larger teams needing multi-brand management and SMS/voice on Pro and Plus.
